results.title: Always on Top Mac: Keep Windows Floating in 2026 (6 Methods)
results.content:
## Method 5: Pin screenshot with ScreenSnap Pro

Here's a method most people overlook: if you need a reference image visible while you work — design specs, instructions, code snippets, color palettes — you can pin a screenshot to your screen.

ScreenSnap Pro includes a Pin Screenshot feature that captures any area of your screen and keeps it floating above all windows. The pinned image stays visible no matter which app you switch to.

How it works:

`⌘ + Shift + 1`

This is perfect for keeping reference material visible — following a tutorial, matching design specs, or comparing layouts. You can annotate the screenshot before pinning it to highlight important details. [...] ScreenSnap Pro

# Always on Top Mac: Keep Windows Floating in 2026 (6 Methods)

Always on top on Mac means pinning a window so it stays visible no matter what else you click. macOS doesn't have a built-in way to do this, but several apps and workarounds make it easy.

Whether you need a reference image floating over your code editor, a chat window visible during presentations, or notes pinned while researching — this guide covers every working method in 2026.

## Why There's No Always on Top Mac Button Built In

Windows has some built-in ways to pin a window on top. macOS doesn't. Apple chose to manage windows through Mission Control, Spaces, and Stage Manager instead. [...] Best for: Power users who want a universal solution plus tons of other productivity features.

## Method 2: Floaty (dedicated floating window app)

Floaty is a small app made just for keeping windows on top on Mac. If you don't need all the extras that come with BetterTouchTool, Floaty is a cleaner pick.

How to use Floaty:

Once you float a window, it stays pinned even when you switch apps. You can float as many windows as you like, and each one will stay above your normal windows.

Floaty also adds a small icon to your menu bar. Click it to see a list of all your floating windows and quickly unpin any of them.

Pros: Simple, works with any window, does one thing and does it well.

Cons: No extra features beyond floating, needs accessibility access to work.

results.title: My app window is stuck halfway off screen how do I fix it? - macOS - TUX Forums
results.content:
It’s happened to me before, super frustrating!  
On Mac, I solved this by clicking on the app icon in the Dock with Option + click, then “Rearrange Windows”.  
Otherwise, going to full screen (Ctrl + Cmd + F) can also force the window to return to the right place.

Powered by Discourse, best viewed with JavaScript enabled [...] # My app window is stuck halfway off screen how do I fix it?

Help! I’ve got this super annoying problem with an app window that’s stuck halfway off my screen. I can only see about half of it, and I can’t figure out how to drag it back down. I’ve tried clicking and dragging the visible part but no luck.

Does anyone know some keyboard tricks to bring a window back into view? Or maybe a way to reset its position or force it into fullscreen mode? This is driving me crazy!

I’m using Mac OS Sequoia if that helps. Thanks in advance to anyone who can save me from this frustration!

I’ve run into this exact problem before - super frustrating!

Try these solutions in order: [...] Terminal Command: If all else fails, open Terminal and paste this command (replacing “AppName” with your app’s name):

`defaults delete com.developer.AppName NSNavLastRootDirectory`

Then restart the app.

Hope one of these works for you! The Option-Click + arrow keys method almost always works for me.

If you’re using macOS Sequoia, try making the app active and pressing Control + Command + F to enter fullscreen mode. Then press the same keys again to exit fullscreen — this often snaps the window back into place. If that doesn’t work, go to System Settings > Displays, change the screen resolution briefly, then switch it back. That usually forces the system to reposition any off-screen windows.
